key_insight: k-means clustering on atomistic fingerprints can pick a small, diverse subset of configurations that fits interatomic potentials more accurately than random selection.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the number of DFT-labeled configurations needed to train reliable classical potentials, lowering data-generation cost for molecular dynamics workflows.
method: Build configuration fingerprints from CrystalNN and radial distribution function (RDF) descriptors
Use k-means to select representative configurations from 1800 Ti structures
Evaluate EAM potential fits against DFT energies and forces, with t-SNE used to visualize overlap and redundancy
evidence_signal: Across varying subset sizes, k-means consistently gave lower errors and standard deviations than random sampling, and about 30 configurations were reported as sufficient for a good fit to the full 1800-configuration set.
watch_item: The approach depends on the chosen fingerprint representation and clustering assumptions, so its performance may vary for other materials, defect types, or potential forms.
paper_title: k-Means Clustering in Fingerprint-Based Configuration Selection for Fitting Interatomic Potentials
